title: convert_response copies the entire upstream body into memory before forwarding
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/proxy.rs:1513-1553, 1285-1313
## Violation
Both convert_response and convert_response_with_usage buffer the entire upstream response via response.bytes().await before constructing the axum Response::Body. For non-streaming responses this is fine. For SSE the proxy never streams — every response is fully buffered. A long-running streaming generation (e.g. claude-opus-4 with 8k tokens, 5 min wall-clock) means the client sits idle for the full generation. Worse, body_max_size is implicit (no cap) so a malicious or buggy upstream that emits megabytes of SSE OOMs the proxy.
## Mandated refactor
For streaming responses (content-type: text/event-stream), use Body::from_stream(response.bytes_stream()) so axum forwards bytes as they arrive. Apply max_response_bytes from config to bound the streaming path too (use a wrapping stream that errors past the limit). Only buffer for non-streaming JSON responses where extract_usage requires the full body.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
